title_zh: 给特朗普账户两声喝彩
rubric_en: The grubby scheme contains the seeds of a good idea
rubric_zh: 这套肮脏方案里却藏着一个好点子

paragraphs.en: Donald Trump likes putting his name on things, from Manhattan skyscrapers (with permission) to Washington’s Kennedy Centre (without). From July 4th Americans have started seeing the president’s name show up in their children’s financial statements. Babies born between 2025 and 2028 are getting “Trump Accounts”: $1,000 invested in American stocks, which will be theirs to spend when they turn 18. Some billionaires and big companies have donated top-ups, and parents can make their own contributions.
paragraphs.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普喜欢把自己的名字贴在各种东西上——从曼哈顿的摩天大楼（经许可）到华盛顿的肯尼迪中心（未获许可）。从7月4日开始，美国人已开始在自己孩子的财务报表上看到总统的名字。2025年至2028年间出生的婴儿将获得“特朗普账户”：每个孩子可获得1000美元的美股投资，在他们18岁时可以支配。部分亿万富翁和大公司已捐款补充，父母也可以自行追加存款。

paragraphs.en: The name is not the only Trumpy thing about the new handouts: they are also partisan, grubby and funded mostly by borrowing. Only babies who are born from 2025 onward, when Mr Trump returned to the White House, will qualify. Roping in America’s wealthiest to chip in alongside the taxpayer carries a whiff of the cronyism that is sadly too familiar in today’s Washington.
paragraphs.zh: 这笔新补助里不止有名字成分有“特朗普味”——它们同样明显带有党派色彩，操作拙劣，而且主要靠举债来支持。只有自2025年起出生的婴儿（也就是特朗普重返白宫后出生的婴儿）才有资格。让美国最富有的人与纳税人一起“掏腰包”，带出一种让人闻到裙带资本主义味道的气息，这在当下华盛顿其实不是什么新鲜景象。

paragraphs.en: However, beneath the unsavoury execution there sits an experiment worth watching. The idea of giving children equity stakes in the economy is sound in principle. It is also well-timed, as societies face up to the looming economic disruption from artificial intelligence.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，在这场不讨喜的执行表面下，确实藏着一个值得关注的实验。把孩子作为“经济股份持有者”来培养，这个想法在原则上是站得住脚的。随着社会正面对人工智能带来的潜在经济冲击，这个想法也很有时机。

paragraphs.en: So far, the scale of this experiment is, admittedly, rather small. The $1,000 each child receives could be worth around $4,500 by the time they turn 18, which is more like $3,000 in today’s prices. Still, it could make a modest but helpful dent in a deposit for a home or car, or in university fees. Many young people already rely on handouts to help buy their first home, but how much they get depends largely on how deep their parents’ pockets are. This undermines the notion that working hard is enough to succeed, a principle without which public support for free markets wanes.
paragraphs.zh: 就目前而言，这项实验的规模确实相当有限。每个孩子收到的1000美元，等到他们18岁时可能增长到约4500美元，按今天的物价约合3000美元。这虽然不算巨额，却可能在购房或买车的首付，或大学学费上，起到一定但有帮助的作用。许多年轻人已经依赖补助来买他们的第一套房，但他们能拿到多少钱，很大程度上取决于父母钱包有多“深”。这会削弱“只要努力就能成功”的理念，而这一理念一旦被削弱，公众对自由市场的支持也会随之下降。

paragraphs.en: Indeed, a troublingly high share of young people profess to feel more enthusiasm for socialism than capitalism. The Economist has spotted a worrying rise in what we call “Gen-Z socialism”, a politics that combines soaking the rich with calls for price controls on everything from rental housing to groceries. Winning the next generation back to the side of free enterprise may require giving them a personal stake in the private sector that goes beyond the labour market.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，相当令人不安的是，令人困扰的是，高比例的年轻人坦言自己对社会主义比对资本主义更有热情。《经济学人》注意到了一股令人担忧的上升趋势，所谓“Z世代社会主义”，这是一种把榨干富人同对从租赁住房到杂货的一切商品施行价格管制的口号捆绑在一起的政治立场。要想让下一代重新回到自由企业这一边，可能需要让他们在私营部门拥有一种超越劳动力市场的个人利益纽带。

paragraphs.en: Compared with other rich countries, America has already done a good job of making investors of everybody: the poorest fifth of Americans have 15% of their assets in stocks, versus just 3% in 1990. Trump Accounts could build on that start by offering a national lesson in financial literacy as children and families watch their money grow.
paragraphs.zh: 与其他富裕国家相比，美国在让人人都能成为投资者方面已经做得相当不错：美国最贫困的五分之一美国人持有15%的资产为股票，而1990年这一比例只有3%。特朗普账户可以在此基础上进一步发展，向儿童和家庭提供一场全国性的金融知识课，让他们亲眼看到自己的资金增长。

paragraphs.en: The experiment is particularly useful preparation for the turmoil that the spread of ai may bring. The more radical forecasts for AI’s economic effects involve vast returns for capital-holders and a tough time for workers. Under that scenario, a direct and highly visible public stake in the technology might be necessary to forestall runaway inequality and political instability.
paragraphs.zh: 这项实验对人工智能扩散可能带来的动荡尤其有备灾意义。对AI经济影响的更激进预测认为，资本持有者将获得巨额回报，而工人将迎来艰难时期。在这种情形下，为了防止失控的不平等和政治动荡，或许有必要通过一种直接且高可见度的公共持股机制来对冲科技带来的冲击。

paragraphs.en: Instead of the government running the show of managing, say, the 5% of OpenAI that Sam Altman has suggested handing over, shares could be passed along to citizens via Trump-like accounts. Even if ai does not make capital eclipse labour, the mechanism could encourage a healthy new form of philanthropy in which some billionaires voluntarily pass on parts of their fortunes to all Americans.
paragraphs.zh: 与其让政府接手管理，例如监管萨姆·阿尔特曼曾建议交出的OpenAI 5%股权，不如可以将股份经由“特朗普式账户”逐步下发给公民。即便AI没有导致资本碾压劳动，該机制也可能推动一种健康的新型慈善实践——一些亿万富翁自愿将自己财富的一部分传递给所有美国人。

paragraphs.en: The scheme’s deficit-financing is not ideal, but at least the borrowing funds savings rather than—as with most government handouts—immediate consumption. In any case, at its current size the effect of the scheme on the deficit is microscopic: 0.005% of annual gdp. So Trump Accounts get two cheers from The Economist. We hope they outlast the president’s term—and that his successor sorts out a name for them that Democrats and Republicans alike can get behind. ■
paragraphs.zh: 这项方案的“赤字融资”并不理想，但至少借款资金是用于储蓄，而不是——和大多数政府福利不同——立即消费。无论如何，就目前规模而言，该方案对财政赤字的影响微乎其微：年国内生产总值的0.005%。因此，《经济学人》对“特朗普账户”给出了两个赞。我们希望它能持续到总统任期结束之后——并且希望他的继任者能为其取一个既能被民主党人也能被共和党人接受的名字。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订阅者可以报名订阅我们的《观点》简报，其中汇集了本刊最佳社论、专栏文章、客座随笔以及读者来稿。
